module-2-volunteer-project-history.label: Module Project: Build Volunteer Project History
module-2-volunteer-project-history.summary: Add one file and record two focused volunteer-project updates.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Add the Meeting-Times File
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Continue `volunteer-project/` from its Module 1 snapshot. Create `meeting-times.txt` with exactly three lines: `Volunteer meeting times`, a blank line, and `Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM`. Save it, stage it, and commit it with the message `Add volunteer meeting times`.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Add only the supplied third file and record it separately.

help.concept: This step reuses the familiar new-file workflow: create the exact text, stage the path, and commit it. The repository grows from two files to three without introducing unrelated content.
help.hint_2: The new commit should add only `meeting-times.txt`.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git status --short
git add meeting-times.txt
git commit -m "Add volunteer meeting times"
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Continue `volunteer-project/` from its Module 1 snapshot. Create `meeting-times.txt` with exactly three lines: `Volunteer meeting times`, a blank line, and `Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM`. Save it, stage it, and commit it with the message `Add volunteer meeting times`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Volunteer coordination notes.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Start volunteer project'
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Volunteer coordination notes.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Volunteer coordination notes.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt

Did you inspect the new meeting-times file?

1.message: Did you stage only the new meeting-times file?

2.message: Did you record the third file as its own commit?

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Inspect the Assignment Change
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Riley has agreed to manage the training desk. Open `volunteer-project/assignments.txt`. Replace `Training desk: unassigned` with `Training desk: Riley`. Save the file, then inspect its unstaged diff.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Make the exact one-line change and review it before staging.

help.concept: `git diff -- assignments.txt` shows the unstaged assignment change and leaves repository state untouched.
help.hint_1: Do not change the welcome-table assignment.
help.hint_2: Leave the edit unstaged for the next step.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Riley has agreed to manage the training desk. Open `volunteer-project/assignments.txt`. Replace `Training desk: unassigned` with `Training desk: Riley`. Save the file, then inspect its unstaged diff.

Did you inspect the assignment change before staging it?

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Stage and Review the Assignment Change
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Stage only `assignments.txt`, then inspect its staged diff.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Prepare and review one focused purpose.

help.concept: `git add assignments.txt` moves the edit into the staging area. `git diff --staged -- assignments.txt` verifies exactly what the next commit would record.
help.hint_1: No other file should be staged.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git add assignments.txt
git diff --staged -- assignments.txt
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Stage only `assignments.txt`, then inspect its staged diff.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Commit the Assignment Change
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Record the staged assignment update with the message `Update volunteer assignments`.

help.concept: `git commit -m "Update volunteer assignments"` records the staged assignment change and leaves the repository ready for the meeting-time update.
help.hint_2: The repository should be clean immediately afterward.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Record the staged assignment update with the message `Update volunteer assignments`.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Move the Orientation Earlier
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: The orientation coordinator moved the session earlier so setup can finish before visitors arrive. Open `volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt`. Replace `Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM` with `Orientation: Saturday 9:30 AM`. Save the file. Inspect the unstaged diff, stage the file, review the staged diff, and commit it with the message `Update volunteer meeting time`.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Follow the complete inspect, stage, review, commit workflow for the exact time change.

help.concept: `git diff` reviews the working-tree edit, `git add` prepares it, `git diff --staged` reviews the proposal, and `git commit` records the focused snapshot.
help.hint_2: The new commit should affect only `meeting-times.txt`.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git diff -- meeting-times.txt
git add meeting-times.txt
git diff --staged -- meeting-times.txt
git commit -m "Update volunteer meeting time"
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: The orientation coordinator moved the session earlier so setup can finish before visitors arrive. Open `volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt`. Replace `Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM` with `Orientation: Saturday 9:30 AM`. Save the file. Inspect the unstaged diff, stage the file, review the staged diff, and commit it with the message `Update volunteer meeting time`.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Audit the Volunteer Project History
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Inspect the concise history, the newest commit summary, and final short status. Confirm that the repository contains four focused commits and no unfinished tracked work.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Verify timeline, latest snapshot, and current state.

help.concept: `git log --oneline` audits the sequence, `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` audits the newest snapshot, and `git status --short` verifies the handoff is clean.
help.hint_1: The newest message should be `Update volunteer meeting time`.
help.hint_2: Short status should produce no file entries.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git log --oneline
git show --stat --oneline HEAD
git status --short
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Inspect the concise history, the newest commit summary, and final short status. Confirm that the repository contains four focused commits and no unfinished tracked work.

moduleProject.description: Continue the same volunteer-project repository. Add one third file, then create two focused text changes without introducing unrelated files.
moduleProject.title: Build Focused Volunteer Project History

intro-sketch.title: Continue the Volunteer Project
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown: The same repository returns with its two-file Module 1 history. Add one exact third file, then create two focused text updates. Each step reuses commands already taught in the guided project, with less command-level guidance.
